name: The Qur'an
description: Verses of mercy, patience, and guidance from the Holy Qur'an.
author: Quips Editorial

quotes.content: Allah tasketh not a soul beyond its scope.
quotes.notes: Opening clause of 2:286; verified verbatim against the Marmaduke Pickthall (1930) translation via al-quran.info.

quotes.content: But lo! with hardship goeth ease,
quotes.notes: The famous 'with hardship comes ease' assurance; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Lo! with hardship goeth ease;
quotes.notes: The repeated assurance that follows 94:5; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Who have believed and whose hearts have rest in the remembrance of Allah. Verily in the remembrance of Allah do hearts find rest!
quotes.notes: On hearts finding rest in remembrance of God; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: O My slaves who have been prodigal to their own hurt! Despair not of the mercy of Allah, Who forgiveth all sins. Lo! He is the Forgiving, the Merciful.
quotes.notes: Excerpt of 39:53 (omitting the opening 'Say:'); verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: O mankind! Lo! We have created you male and female, and have made you nations and tribes that ye may know one another.
quotes.notes: On human diversity and mutual understanding; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Thy Lord hath decreed, that ye worship none save Him, and (that ye show) kindness to parents.
quotes.notes: Opening excerpt of 17:23 on kindness to parents; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And lower unto them the wing of submission through mercy, and say: My Lord! Have mercy on them both as they did care for me when I was little.
quotes.notes: A prayer of mercy for one's parents; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Therefore remember Me, I will remember you. Give thanks to Me, and reject not Me.
quotes.notes: On remembrance and gratitude; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And when your Lord proclaimed: If ye give thanks, I will give you more; but if ye are thankless, lo! My punishment is dire.
quotes.notes: On gratitude bringing increase; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And whosoever putteth his trust in Allah, He will suffice him. Lo! Allah bringeth His command to pass. Allah hath set a measure for all things.
quotes.notes: Excerpt of 65:3 on reliance upon God; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Faint not nor grieve, for ye will overcome them if ye are (indeed) believers.
quotes.notes: An exhortation against despair; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: O ye who believe! Seek help in steadfastness and prayer. Lo! Allah is with the steadfast.
quotes.notes: On patience (sabr) and prayer; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And when My servants question thee concerning Me, then surely I am nigh. I answer the prayer of the suppliant when he crieth unto Me.
quotes.notes: On God's nearness to those who call on Him; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And call on Him in fear and hope. Lo! the mercy of Allah is nigh unto the good.
quotes.notes: Closing excerpt of 7:56 on mercy; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Thy Lord hath not forsaken thee nor doth He hate thee,
quotes.notes: A verse of reassurance in the morning; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And verily the latter portion will be better for thee than the former,
quotes.notes: The promise that what is to come is better; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Is the reward of goodness aught save goodness?
quotes.notes: On goodness returned for goodness; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Lo! Allah enjoineth justice and kindness, and giving to kinsfolk, and forbiddeth lewdness and abomination and wickedness.
quotes.notes: Excerpt of 16:90 on justice and kindness; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: The good deed and the evil deed are not alike. Repel the evil deed with one which is better, then lo! he, between whom and thee there was enmity (will become) as though he was a bosom friend.
quotes.notes: On answering evil with good; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: O ye who believe! Endure, outdo all others in endurance, be ready, and observe your duty to Allah, in order that ye may succeed.
quotes.notes: On endurance and steadfastness; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: As for those who strive in Us, We surely guide them to Our paths, and lo! Allah is with the good.
quotes.notes: On divine guidance for those who strive; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: The (faithful) slaves of the Beneficent are they who walk upon the earth modestly, and when the foolish ones address them answer: Peace;
quotes.notes: On humility and answering with peace; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: No calamity befalleth save by Allah's leave. And whosoever believeth in Allah, He guideth his heart. And Allah is Knower of all things.
quotes.notes: On God guiding the heart through trial; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And verily whoso is patient and forgiveth - lo! that, verily, is (of) the steadfast heart of things.
quotes.notes: On patience and forgiveness; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: O mankind! There hath come unto you an exhortation from your Lord, a balm for that which is in the breasts, a guidance and a mercy for believers.
quotes.notes: On the Qur'an as a balm and mercy; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Seek help in patience and prayer; and truly it is hard save for the humble-minded,
quotes.notes: On patience, prayer, and humility; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And your Lord hath said: Pray unto Me and I will hear your prayer.
quotes.notes: Opening excerpt of 40:60 on God answering prayer; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Allah! There is no deity save Him, the Alive, the Eternal. Neither slumber nor sleep overtaketh Him.
quotes.notes: Opening excerpt of the Throne Verse (Ayat al-Kursi); verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: So pardon them and ask forgiveness for them and consult with them upon the conduct of affairs. And when thou art resolved, then put thy trust in Allah. Lo! Allah loveth those who put their trust (in Him).
quotes.notes: Excerpt of 3:159 on pardon, counsel, and trust; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: Your Lord hath prescribed for Himself mercy,
quotes.notes: Excerpt of 6:54 on God's self-appointed mercy; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.

quotes.content: And surely We shall try you with something of fear and hunger, and loss of wealth and lives and crops; but give glad tidings to the steadfast,
quotes.notes: On glad tidings for the patient amid trial; verified verbatim against the Pickthall translation.
